revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''松明あかし'''（たいまつあかし）とは、[[福島県]][[須賀川市]]で420年続く[[火祭り]]である。毎年[[11月]]の第二土曜日...'
revision.text:
'''松明あかし'''（たいまつあかし）とは、[[福島県]][[須賀川市]]で420年続く[[火祭り]]である。毎年[[11月]]の第二土曜日に開催される。
== 歴史 == *[[2011年]]の[[福島第一原子力発電所事故]]の影響により、祭に使用する地元産の[[カヤ]]から微量の[[放射性物質]]が検出された。須賀川市は[[福島第一原子力発電所|原発]]から約60kmの所に位置しており、全国に材料の提供を求めた<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 『福島・須賀川で「松明あかし」 全国から材料の支援』]（[[asahi.com]] 2011年11月12日）</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 『復興の炎、天まで届け…福島で「松明あかし」』]（[[YOMIURI ONLINE]] 2011年11月13日）</ref> 。
==交通アクセス== *[[須賀川市]]までは[[新幹線]]で[[東京]]から約1時間、[[仙台]]・[[宇都宮]]からは約1時間40分。 *[[JR]][[須賀川駅]]から徒歩15分 *[[東北自動車道]]の[[須賀川インターチェンジ]]から車で5分 *会場周辺に駐車場が約2,000台分ある
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[火祭り]] *[[須賀川市|福島県須賀川市]] *[[日本三大一覧]] 「[[日本三大一覧#祭り|日本三大火祭り]]」
==外部リンク== *[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/taimatsu/index.html 松明あかし（須賀川市）]
{{DEFAULTSORT:たいまつあかし}} [[Category:福島県の祭り]] [[Category:南部町 (山梨県)]] {{event-stub}}

画像提供依頼のcat変更, cl
revision.text:
{{画像提供依頼|松明あかし|祭りの様子|date=2011年11月|cat=須賀川市}} '''松明あかし'''（たいまつあかし）とは、[[福島県]][[須賀川市]]で420年続く[[火祭り]]である。毎年[[11月]]の第2土曜日に開催される。
== 歴史 == * [[1985年]]までは新暦の[[11月10日]]に開催されていたが、[[1986年]]以降は現在の11月第2土曜日に開催されている。 * [[1995年]]は「ふくしま国体」の開催に合わせて、通常より1か月早い[[10月14日]]（土）に開催された。 * [[2011年]]の[[福島第一原子力発電所事故]]の影響により、祭に使用する地元産の[[カヤ]]から微量の[[放射性物質]]が検出された。須賀川市は[[福島第一原子力発電所|原発]]から約60kmの所に位置しており、全国に材料の提供を求めた<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 『福島・須賀川で「松明あかし」 全国から材料の支援』]（[[asahi.com]] 2011年11月12日）</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 『復興の炎、天まで届け…福島で「松明あかし」』]（[[YOMIURI ONLINE]] 2011年11月13日）</ref> 。
== 交通アクセス == * [[須賀川市]]までは[[新幹線]]で[[東京]]から約1時間、[[仙台]]・[[宇都宮]]からは約1時間40分。 * [[JR]][[須賀川駅]]から徒歩15分 * [[東北自動車道]]の[[須賀川インターチェンジ]]から車で5分 * 会場周辺に駐車場が約2,000台分ある
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[火祭り]] * [[須賀川市|福島県須賀川市]] * [[日本三大一覧]] 「[[日本三大一覧#祭り|日本三大火祭り]]」
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/taimatsu/ 松明あかし（須賀川市）]
{{Event-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たいまつあかし}} [[Category:福島県の祭り]] [[Category:須賀川市]]

{{画像提供依頼|松明あかし|祭りの様子|date=2011年11月|cat=須賀川市}} '''松明あかし'''（たいまつあかし）とは、[[福島県]][[須賀川市]]で420年続く[[火祭り]]である。日本三大[[火祭り]]の一つに数えられることがある。毎年[[11月]]の第2土曜日に開催される。
== 概要 == [[伊達政宗]]の軍が、[[須賀川城]]の[[二階堂氏]]を攻め滅ぼした際の故事に由来。長さ10m、重さ3トンもの巨大な[[松明]]30本が市内を練り歩いた後、会場の五老山（翠ヶ丘公園内）に立てられ、火を灯される<ref>[http://syeg.jp/taimatsu/ 須賀川市商工会議所青年部 - 松明あかし関連事業]</ref>。
== 歴史 == * [[1589年]]（[[天正]]17年）伊達政宗が[[岩瀬郡]]周辺に勢力を伸ばして須賀川城の二階堂氏にも降伏を迫った。これに怒った家臣や領民が[[10月10日]]の夜、手に松明をともして町の東にある十日山に集まり、決死の覚悟で城を守ることを決議して城主の大乗院に進言した。この故事が祭りの元となる。 * 須賀川城の落城後は、この戦いで戦死した多くの人々の霊を弔うための行事として祭りが続けられた。須賀川周辺が新しい領主の支配下に入った後は領主の目をはばかり、ムジナ狩りと称して続けられ<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm 須賀川観光協会 - 松明あかしの由来]</ref>、現在に至る。 * かつては旧暦の10月10日、その後は[[1985年]]までは新暦の[[11月10日]]に開催されていたが、[[1986年]]以降は現在の11月第2土曜日に開催されている。 * [[1995年]]は「ふくしま国体」の開催に合わせて、通常より1か月早い[[10月14日]]（土）に開催された。 * [[2011年]]の[[福島第一原子力発電所事故]]の影響により、祭に使用する地元産の[[カヤ]]から微量の[[放射性物質]]が検出された。須賀川市は[[福島第一原子力発電所|原発]]から約60kmの所に位置しており、全国に材料の提供を求めた<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 『福島・須賀川で「松明あかし」 全国から材料の支援』]（[[asahi.com]] 2011年11月12日）</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 『復興の炎、天まで届け…福島で「松明あかし」』]（[[YOMIURI ONLINE]] 2011年11月13日）</ref> 。
== 交通アクセス == * [[須賀川市]]までは[[東北新幹線]][[郡山駅 (福島県)|郡山]]経由で[[東京]]から約1時間、[[仙台]]・[[宇都宮]]からは約1時間40分。 * [[JR]][[須賀川駅]]から徒歩15分 * [[東北自動車道]]の[[須賀川インターチェンジ]]から車で5分 * 会場周辺に駐車場が約2,000台分ある
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[火祭り]] * [[須賀川市|福島県須賀川市]] * [[日本三大一覧]] 「[[日本三大一覧#祭り|日本三大火祭り]]」
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/taimatsu/ 松明あかし（須賀川市）]
{{Event-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たいまつあかし}} [[Category:福島県の祭り]] [[Category:須賀川市]]

== 交通アクセス == * [[須賀川市]]までは[[東北新幹線]][[郡山駅 (福島県)|郡山]]経由で[[東京]]から約1時間、[[仙台]]・[[宇都宮]]からは約1時間40分。 * [[JR]][[須賀川駅]]から徒歩15分 * [[東北自動車道]]の[[須賀川インターチェンジ]]から車で5分 * 会場周辺に駐車場が約2,000台分ある
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[火祭り]] * [[須賀川市|福島県須賀川市]] * [[日本三大一覧]] 「[[日本三大一覧#祭り|日本三大火祭り]]」
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/taimatsu/ 松明あかし（須賀川市）]
{{Event-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たいまつあかし}} [[Category:福島県の祭り]] [[Category:須賀川市]]

[[File:Kuriyasawa, Sukagawa, Fukushima Prefecture 962-0866, Japan - panoramio.jpg|thumb|280px|松明あかし]] '''松明あかし'''（たいまつあかし）とは、[[福島県]][[須賀川市]]で420年続く[[火祭り]]である。日本三大[[火祭り]]の一つに数えられることがある。毎年[[11月]]の第2土曜日に開催される。
== 概要 == [[伊達政宗]]の軍が、[[須賀川城]]の[[二階堂氏]]を攻め滅ぼした際の故事に由来。長さ10m、重さ3トンもの巨大な[[松明]]30本が市内を練り歩いた後、会場の五老山（[[翠ヶ丘公園]]内）に立てられ、火を灯される<ref>[http://syeg.jp/taimatsu/ 須賀川市商工会議所青年部 - 松明あかし関連事業]</ref>。
== 歴史 == * [[1589年]]（[[天正]]17年）伊達政宗が[[岩瀬郡]]周辺に勢力を伸ばして須賀川城の二階堂氏にも降伏を迫った。これに怒った家臣や領民が[[10月10日]]の夜、手に松明をともして町の東にある十日山に集まり、決死の覚悟で城を守ることを決議して城主の[[阿南姫|大乗院]]に進言した。この故事が祭りの元となる。 * 須賀川城の落城後は、この戦いで戦死した多くの人々の霊を弔うための行事として祭りが続けられた。須賀川周辺が新しい領主の支配下に入った後は領主の目をはばかり、ムジナ狩りと称して続けられ<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm 須賀川観光協会 - 松明あかしの由来]</ref>、現在に至る。 * かつては旧暦の10月10日、その後は[[1985年]]までは新暦の[[11月10日]]に開催されていたが、[[1986年]]以降は現在の11月第2土曜日に開催されている。 * [[1995年]]は「ふくしま国体」の開催に合わせて、通常より1か月早い[[10月14日]]に開催された。 * [[2011年]]の[[福島第一原子力発電所事故]]の影響により、祭に使用する地元産の[[カヤ]]から微量の[[放射性物質]]が検出された。須賀川市は[[福島第一原子力発電所|原発]]から約60kmの所に位置しており、全国に材料の提供を求めた<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 『福島・須賀川で「松明あかし」 全国から材料の支援』]（[[asahi.com]] 2011年11月12日）</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 『復興の炎、天まで届け…福島で「松明あかし」』]（[[YOMIURI ONLINE]] 2011年11月13日）</ref> 。
== 交通アクセス == * [[須賀川市]]までは[[東北新幹線]][[郡山駅 (福島県)|郡山]]経由で[[東京]]から約1時間、[[仙台]]・[[宇都宮]]からは約1時間40分。 * [[JR]][[須賀川駅]]から徒歩15分 * [[東北自動車道]]の[[須賀川インターチェンジ]]から車で5分 * 会場周辺に駐車場が約2,000台分ある
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[火祭り]] * [[須賀川市|福島県須賀川市]] * [[日本三大一覧]] 「[[日本三大一覧#祭り|日本三大火祭り]]」
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/taimatsu/ 松明あかし（須賀川市）]
{{Event-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たいまつあかし}} [[Category:福島県の祭り]] [[Category:須賀川市]]

== 概要 == [http://syeg.jp/taimatsu/ 須賀川市商工]
== 歴史 == * [[1589年]]（[[天正]]17年）伊達政宗が[[岩瀬郡]]周辺に勢力を伸ばして須賀川城の二階堂氏にも降伏を迫った。これに怒った家臣や領民が[[10月10日]]の夜、手に松明をともして町の東にある十日山に集まり、決死の覚悟で城を守ることを決議して城主の[[阿南姫|大乗院]]に進言した。この故事が祭りの元となる。 * 須賀川城の落城後は、この戦いで戦死した多くの人々の霊を弔うための行事として祭りが続けられた。須賀川周辺が新しい領主の支配下に入った後は領主の目をはばかり、ムジナ狩りと称して続けられ<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm 須賀川観光協会 - 松明あかしの由来]</ref>、現在に至る。 * かつては旧暦の10月10日、その後は[[1985年]]までは新暦の[[11月10日]]に開催されていたが、[[1986年]]以降は現在の11月第2土曜日に開催されている。 * [[1995年]]は「ふくしま国体」の開催に合わせて、通常より1か月早い[[10月14日]]に開催された。 * [[2011年]]の[[福島第一原子力発電所事故]]の影響により、祭に使用する地元産の[[カヤ]]から微量の[[放射性物質]]が検出された。須賀川市は[[福島第一原子力発電所|原発]]から約60kmの所に位置しており、全国に材料の提供を求めた<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 『福島・須賀川で「松明あかし」 全国から材料の支援』]（[[asahi.com]] 2011年11月12日）</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 『復興の炎、天まで届け…福島で「松明あかし」』]（[[YOMIURI ONLINE]] 2011年11月13日）</ref> 。 * [[2020年]]・[[2021年]]は新型コロナウイルス感染拡大防止のため無観客で実施している。

[[File:Kuriyasawa, Sukagawa, Fukushima Prefecture 962-0866, Japan - panoramio.jpg|thumb|280px|松明あかし]] '''松明あかし'''（たいまつあかし）とは、[[福島県]][[須賀川市]]で430年続く[[火祭り]]である。日本三大[[火祭り]]の一つに数えられることがある。毎年[[11月]]の第2土曜日に開催される。

== 歴史 == * [[1589年]]（[[天正]]17年）[[伊達政宗]]が[[岩瀬郡]]周辺に勢力を伸ばして[[須賀川城]]の[[二階堂氏]]にも降伏を迫った。これに怒った家臣や領民が[[10月10日]]の夜、手に松明をともして町の東にある十日山に集まり、決死の覚悟で城を守ることを決議して城主の[[阿南姫|大乗院]]に進言した。この[[故事]]が祭りの元となる。 * 須賀川城の落城後は、この戦いで戦死した多くの人々の霊を弔うための行事として祭りが続けられた。須賀川周辺が新しい領主の支配下に入った後は領主の目をはばかり、ムジナ狩りと称して続けられ<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm 須賀川観光協会 - 松明あかしの由来]</ref>、現在に至る。 * かつては旧暦の10月10日、その後は[[1985年]]までは新暦の[[11月10日]]に開催されていたが、[[1986年]]以降は現在の11月第2土曜日に開催されている。 * 御神火は[[二階堂神社]]で採火され、須賀川市内を巡り、会場の翠ヶ丘公園へ運ばれる。 * [[1995年]]は「ふくしま国体」の開催に合わせて、通常より1か月早い[[10月14日]]に開催された。 * [[2011年]]の[[福島第一原子力発電所事故]]の影響により、祭に使用する地元産の[[カヤ]]から微量の[[放射性物質]]が検出された。須賀川市は[[福島第一原子力発電所|原発]]から約60kmの所に位置しており、全国に材料の提供を求めた<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 『福島・須賀川で「松明あかし」 全国から材料の支援』]（[[asahi.com]] 2011年11月12日）</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 『復興の炎、天まで届け…福島で「松明あかし」』]（[[YOMIURI ONLINE]] 2011年11月13日）</ref> 。 * [[2020年]]・[[2021年]]は新型コロナウイルス感染拡大防止のため無観客で実施している。

== 概要 == [http://syeg.jp/taimatsu/ 須賀川市商工]
== 歴史 == * [[1589年]]（[[天正]]17年）[[伊達政宗]]が[[岩瀬郡]]周辺に勢力を伸ばして[[須賀川城]]の[[二階堂氏]]にも降伏を迫った。これに怒った家臣や領民が[[10月10日]]の夜、手に松明をともして町の東にある十日山に集まり、決死の覚悟で城を守ることを決議して城主の[[阿南姫|大乗院]]に進言した。この[[故事]]が祭りの元となる。 * 須賀川城の落城後は、この戦いで戦死した多くの人々の霊を弔うための行事として祭りが続けられた。須賀川周辺が新しい領主の支配下に入った後は領主の目をはばかり、ムジナ狩りと称して続けられ<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm 須賀川観光協会 - 松明あかしの由来]</ref>、現在に至る。 * かつては旧暦の10月10日、その後は[[1985年]]までは新暦の[[11月10日]]に開催されていたが、[[1986年]]以降は現在の11月第2土曜日に開催されている。 * 御神火は[[二階堂神社]]で採火され、須賀川市内を巡り、会場の翠ヶ丘公園へ運ばれる。 * [[1995年]]は「ふくしま国体」の開催に合わせて、通常より1か月早い[[10月14日]]に開催された。 * [[2011年]]の[[福島第一原子力発電所事故]]の影響により、祭に使用する地元産の[[カヤ]]から微量の[[放射性物質]]が検出された。須賀川市は[[福島第一原子力発電所|原発]]から約60kmの所に位置しており、全国に材料の提供を求めた<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 『福島・須賀川で「松明あかし」 全国から材料の支援』]（[[asahi.com]] 2011年11月12日）</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 『復興の炎、天まで届け…福島で「松明あかし」』]（[[YOMIURI ONLINE]] 2011年11月13日）</ref> 。 * [[2020年]]・[[2021年]]は新型コロナウイルス感染拡大防止のため無観客で実施している。
== 交通アクセス == * [[須賀川市]]までは[[東北新幹線]][[郡山駅 (福島県)|郡山]]経由で[[東京]]から約1時間、[[仙台]]・[[宇都宮市|宇都宮]]からは約1時間40分。 * [[JR]][[須賀川駅]]から徒歩15分 * [[東北自動車道]]の[[須賀川インターチェンジ]]から車で5分 * 会場周辺に駐車場が約2,000台分ある
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[火祭り]] * [[須賀川市|福島県須賀川市]] * [[日本三大一覧]] 「[[日本三大一覧#祭り|日本三大火祭り]]」
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/taimatsu/ 松明あかし（須賀川市）]
{{Event-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たいまつあかし}} [[Category:福島県の祭り]] [[Category:須賀川市]]

revision.text:
[[File:Kuriyasawa, Sukagawa, Fukushima Prefecture 962-0866, Japan - panoramio.jpg|thumb|280px|Taimatsu Akashi]] '''Taimatsu Akashi''' (松明あかし) is a {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, with a history spanning over 430 years. It is sometimes counted among {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り}}. The festival takes place annually on the second Saturday of {{ill|November|ja|11月}}.
== Overview == The festival is organized by the Sukagawa City Chamber of Commerce and features large {{ill|torches|ja|松明}} lit as a prayer for protection against disasters and a memorial for those who perished in historical battles.
== History == * In {{ill|1589|ja|1589年}} ({{ill|Tenshō 17|ja|天正17年}}), {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}} expanded his influence into the {{ill|Iwase District|ja|岩瀬郡}} region and demanded the surrender of the {{ill|Nikaidō clan|ja|二階堂氏}} at {{ill|Sukagawa Castle|ja|須賀川城}}. Enraged by this, the retainers and residents gathered on October 10th with torches in hand at {{ill|Tokayama|ja|十日山}}, east of the town, pledging to defend the castle at all costs. This event is considered the origin of the festival. * After the fall of Sukagawa Castle, the festival evolved into a ceremony to console the spirits of those who died in battle. Under new feudal rule, the festival was disguised as a "Mujina Hunt" to avoid interference from the rulers.<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm Sukagawa Tourism Association - Origin of Taimatsu Akashi]</ref> * The festival was historically held on October 10th of the lunar calendar and later shifted to November 10th of the solar calendar. Since {{ill|1986|ja|1986年}}, it has been held on the second Saturday of November. * The sacred fire is taken from {{ill|Nikaido Shrine|ja|二階堂神社}} and carried through Sukagawa City to the event site at {{ill|Midori-gaoka Park|ja|翠ヶ丘公園}}. * In {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}}, the festival was held a month earlier on October 14th to coincide with the "Fukushima Kokutai" event. * In {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}, due to the effects of the {{ill|Fukushima nuclear accident|ja|福島第一原子力発電所事故}}, local {{ill|Torreya nucifera|lt=kaya grass|ja|カヤ}} used for the torches was found to contain trace amounts of radioactive material. Materials were sourced from across Japan to continue the festival.<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 'Fukushima's Sukagawa Hosts Taimatsu Akashi with Nationwide Material Support'] (Asahi.com, November 12, 2011)</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 'Flames of Recovery Brighten Fukushima - Taimatsu Akashi Held'] (Yomiuri Online, November 13, 2011)</ref> * The {{ill|2020|ja|2020年}} and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}} festivals were held without spectators to prevent the spread of COVID-19.
== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}} via the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}} (transfer at {{ill|Koriyama Station (Fukushima)|ja|郡山駅}}), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台}} or {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Sukagawa Station|ja|須賀川駅}} (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}}. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Fire festival|ja|火祭り}} * {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|福島県須賀川市}} * {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/taimatsu/ Taimatsu Akashi (Sukagawa City)]
{{Event-stub}}

== History == * In {{ill|1589|ja|1589年}} ({{ill|Tenshō 17|ja|天正17年}}), {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}} expanded his influence into the {{ill|Iwase District|ja|岩瀬郡}} region and demanded the surrender of the {{ill|Nikaidō clan|ja|二階堂氏}} at {{ill|Sukagawa Castle|ja|須賀川城}}. Enraged by this, the retainers and residents gathered on October 10th with torches in hand at {{ill|Tokayama|ja|十日山}}, east of the town, pledging to defend the castle at all costs. This event is considered the origin of the festival. * After the fall of Sukagawa Castle, the festival evolved into a ceremony to console the spirits of those who died in battle. Under new feudal rule, the festival was disguised as a "Mujina Hunt" to avoid interference from the rulers.<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm Sukagawa Tourism Association - Origin of Taimatsu Akashi]</ref> * The festival was historically held on October 10th of the lunar calendar and later shifted to November 10th of the solar calendar. [[File:Kuriyasawa, Sukagawa, Fukushima Prefecture 962-0866, Japan - panoramio.jpg|thumb|280px|Taimatsu Akashi]] '''Taimatsu Akashi''' (松明あかし) is a {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, with a history spanning over 430 years. It is sometimes counted among {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り}}. The festival takes place annually on the second Saturday of {{ill|November|ja|11月}}.
== Overview == The festival is organized by the Sukagawa City Chamber of Commerce and features large {{ill|torches|ja|松明}} lit as a prayer for protection against disasters and a memorial for those who perished in historical battles.
== History == * In {{ill|1589|ja|1589年}} ({{ill|Tenshō 17|ja|天正17年}}), {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}} expanded his influence into the {{ill|Iwase District|ja|岩瀬郡}} region and demanded the surrender of the {{ill|Nikaidō clan|ja|二階堂氏}} at {{ill|Sukagawa Castle|ja|須賀川城}}. Enraged by this, the retainers and residents gathered on October 10th with torches in hand at {{ill|Tokayama|ja|十日山}}, east of the town, pledging to defend the castle at all costs. This event is considered the origin of the festival. * After the fall of Sukagawa Castle, the festival evolved into a ceremony to console the spirits of those who died in battle. Under new feudal rule, the festival was disguised as a "Mujina Hunt" to avoid interference from the rulers.<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm Sukagawa Tourism Association - Origin of Taimatsu Akashi]</ref> * The festival was historically held on October 10th of the lunar calendar and later shifted to November 10th of the solar calendar. Since {{ill|1986|ja|1986年}}, it has been held on the second Saturday of November. * The sacred fire is taken from {{ill|Nikaido Shrine|ja|二階堂神社}} and carried through Sukagawa City to the event site at {{ill|Midori-gaoka Park|ja|翠ヶ丘公園}}. * In {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}}, the festival was held a month earlier on October 14th to coincide with the "Fukushima Kokutai" event. * In {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}, due to the effects of the {{ill|Fukushima nuclear accident|ja|福島第一原子力発電所事故}}, local {{ill|Torreya nucifera|lt=kaya grass|ja|カヤ}} used for the torches was found to contain trace amounts of radioactive material. Materials were sourced from across Japan to continue the festival.<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 'Fukushima's Sukagawa Hosts Taimatsu Akashi with Nationwide Material Support'] (Asahi.com, November 12, 2011)</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 'Flames of Recovery Brighten Fukushima - Taimatsu Akashi Held'] (Yomiuri Online, November 13, 2011)</ref> * The {{ill|2020|ja|2020年}} and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}} festivals were held without spectators to prevent the spread of COVID-19.
== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}} via the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}} (transfer at {{ill|Koriyama Station (Fukushima)|ja|郡山駅}}), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台}} or {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Sukagawa Station|ja|須賀川駅}} (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}}. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Fire festival|ja|火祭り}} * {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|福島県須賀川市}} * {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/taimatsu/ Taimatsu Akashi (Sukagawa City)]
{{Event-stub}}

== History == * In {{ill|1589|ja|1589年}} ({{ill|Tenshō 17|ja|天正17年}}), {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}} expanded his influence into the {{ill|Iwase District|ja|岩瀬郡}} region and demanded the surrender of the {{ill|Nikaidō clan|ja|二階堂氏}} at {{ill|Sukagawa Castle|ja|須賀川城}}. Under new feudal rule, the festival was disguised as a "Mujina Hunt" to avoid interference from the rulers.<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm Sukagawa Tourism Association - Origin of Taimatsu Akashi]</ref> * The festival was historically held on October 10th of the lunar calendar and later shifted to November 10th of the solar calendar. This event is considered the origin of the festival. * After the fall of Sukagawa Castle, the festival evolved into a ceremony to console the spirits of those who died in battle. Under new feudal rule, the festival was disguised as a "Mujina Hunt" to avoid interference from the rulers.<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm Sukagawa Tourism Association - Origin of Taimatsu Akashi]</ref> * The festival was historically held on October 10th of the lunar calendar and later shifted to November 10th of the solar calendar. This event is considered the origin of the festival. * After the fall of Sukagawa Castle, the festival evolved into a ceremony to console the spirits of those who died in battle. Under new feudal rule, the festival was disguised as a "Mujina Hunt" to avoid interference from the rulers.<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm Sukagawa Tourism Association - Origin of Taimatsu Akashi]</ref> * The festival was historically held on October 10th of the lunar calendar and later shifted to November 10th of the solar calendar. Since {{ill|1986|ja|1986年}}, it has been held on the second Saturday of November. * The sacred fire is taken from {{ill|Nikaido Shrine|ja|二階堂神社}} and carried through Sukagawa City to the event site at {{ill|Midori-gaoka Park|ja|翠ヶ丘公園}}. * In {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}}, the festival was held a month earlier on October 14th to coincide with the "Fukushima Kokutai" event. * In {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}, due to the effects of the {{ill|Fukushima nuclear accident|ja|福島第一原子力発電所事故}}, local {{ill|Torreya nucifera|lt=kaya grass|ja|カヤ}} used for the torches was found to contain trace amounts of radioactive material. Materials were sourced from across Japan to continue the festival.<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 'Fukushima's Sukagawa Hosts Taimatsu Akashi with Nationwide Material Support'] (Asahi.com, November 12, 2011)</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 'Flames of Recovery Brighten Fukushima - Taimatsu Akashi Held'] (Yomiuri Online, November 13, 2011)</ref> * The {{ill|2020|ja|2020年}} and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}} festivals were held without spectators to prevent the spread of COVID-19.
== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}} via the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}} (transfer at {{ill|Koriyama Station (Fukushima)|ja|郡山駅}}), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台}} or {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Sukagawa Station|ja|須賀川駅}} (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}}. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.

[[File:Kuriyasawa, Sukagawa, Fukushima Prefecture 962-0866, Japan - panoramio.jpg|thumb|280px|Taimatsu Akashi]] '''Taimatsu Akashi''' (松明あかし) is a {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, with a history spanning over 430 years. It is sometimes counted among {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り}}. The festival takes place annually on the second Saturday of {{ill|November|ja|11月}}.
== Overview == The festival is organized by the Sukagawa City Chamber of Commerce and features large {{ill|torches|ja|松明}} lit as a prayer for protection against disasters and a memorial for those who perished in historical battles.
== History == * In {{ill|1589|ja|1589年}} ({{ill|Tenshō 17|ja|天正17年}}), {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}} expanded his influence into the {{ill|Iwase District|ja|岩瀬郡}} region and demanded the surrender of the {{ill|Nikaidō clan|ja|二階堂氏}} at {{ill|Sukagawa Castle|ja|須賀川城}}. Enraged by this, the retainers and residents gathered on October 10th with torches in hand at {{ill|Tokayama|ja|十日山}}, east of the town, pledging to defend the castle at all costs. This event is considered the origin of the festival. * After the fall of Sukagawa Castle, the festival evolved into a ceremony to console the spirits of those who died in battle. Under new feudal rule, the festival was disguised as a "Mujina Hunt" to avoid interference from the rulers.<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm Sukagawa Tourism Association - Origin of Taimatsu Akashi]</ref> * The festival was historically held on October 10th of the lunar calendar and later shifted to November 10th of the solar calendar. Since {{ill|1986|ja|1986年}}, it has been held on the second Saturday of November. * The sacred fire is taken from {{ill|Nikaido Shrine|ja|二階堂神社}} and carried through Sukagawa City to the event site at {{ill|Midori-gaoka Park|ja|翠ヶ丘公園}}. * In {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}}, the festival was held a month earlier on October 14th to coincide with the "Fukushima Kokutai" event. * In {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}, due to the effects of the {{ill|Fukushima nuclear accident|ja|福島第一原子力発電所事故}}, local {{ill|Torreya nucifera|lt=kaya grass|ja|カヤ}} used for the torches was found to contain trace amounts of radioactive material. Materials were sourced from across Japan to continue the festival.<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 'Fukushima's Sukagawa Hosts Taimatsu Akashi with Nationwide Material Support'] (Asahi.com, November 12, 2011)</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 'Flames of Recovery Brighten Fukushima - Taimatsu Akashi Held'] (Yomiuri Online, November 13, 2011)</ref> * The {{ill|2020|ja|2020年}} and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}} festivals were held without spectators to prevent the spread of COVID-19.
== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}} via the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}} (transfer at {{ill|Koriyama Station (Fukushima)|ja|郡山駅}}), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台}} or {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Sukagawa Station|ja|須賀川駅}} (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}}. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Fire festival|ja|火祭り}} * {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|福島県須賀川市}} * {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
[[File:Kuriyasawa, Sukagawa, Fukushima Prefecture 962-0866, Japan - panoramio.jpg|thumb|280px|Taimatsu Akashi]] '''Taimatsu Akashi''' (松明あかし) is a {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, with a history spanning over 430 years. It is sometimes counted among {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り}}. The festival takes place annually on the second Saturday of {{ill|November|ja|11月}}.
== Overview == The festival is organized by the Sukagawa City Chamber of Commerce and features large {{ill|torches|ja|松明}} lit as a prayer for protection against disasters and a memorial for those who perished in historical battles.
== History == * In {{ill|1589|ja|1589年}} ({{ill|Tenshō 17|ja|天正17年}}), {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}} expanded his influence into the {{ill|Iwase District|ja|岩瀬郡}} region and demanded the surrender of the {{ill|Nikaidō clan|ja|二階堂氏}} at {{ill|Sukagawa Castle|ja|須賀川城}}. Enraged by this, the retainers and residents gathered on October 10th with torches in hand at {{ill|Tokayama|ja|十日山}}, east of the town, pledging to defend the castle at all costs. This event is considered the origin of the festival. * After the fall of Sukagawa Castle, the festival evolved into a ceremony to console the spirits of those who died in battle. Under new feudal rule, the festival was disguised as a "Mujina Hunt" to avoid interference from the rulers.<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm Sukagawa Tourism Association - Origin of Taimatsu Akashi]</ref> * The festival was historically held on October 10th of the lunar calendar and later shifted to November 10th of the solar calendar. Since {{ill|1986|ja|1986年}}, it has been held on the second Saturday of November. * The sacred fire is taken from {{ill|Nikaido Shrine|ja|二階堂神社}} and carried through Sukagawa City to the event site at {{ill|Midori-gaoka Park|ja|翠ヶ丘公園}}. * In {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}}, the festival was held a month earlier on October 14th to coincide with the "Fukushima Kokutai" event. * In {{ill|2011|ja|2011年}}, due to the effects of the {{ill|Fukushima nuclear accident|ja|福島第一原子力発電所事故}}, local {{ill|Torreya nucifera|lt=kaya grass|ja|カヤ}} used for the torches was found to contain trace amounts of radioactive material. Materials were sourced from across Japan to continue the festival.<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 'Fukushima's Sukagawa Hosts Taimatsu Akashi with Nationwide Material Support'] (Asahi.com, November 12, 2011)</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 'Flames of Recovery Brighten Fukushima - Taimatsu Akashi Held'] (Yomiuri Online, November 13, 2011)</ref> * The {{ill|2020|ja|2020年}} and {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}} festivals were held without spectators to prevent the spread of COVID-19.
== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}} via the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}} (transfer at {{ill|Koriyama Station (Fukushima)|ja|郡山駅}}), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台}} or {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Sukagawa Station|ja|須賀川駅}} (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}}. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also! == * {{ill|Fire festival|ja|火祭り}} * {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|福島県須賀川市}} * {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/taimatsu/ Taimatsu Akashi (Sukagawa City)]
{{Event-stub}}

Since {{ill|1986|ja|1986年}}, it has been held on the second Saturday of November. * The sacred fire is taken from {{ill|Nikaido Shrine|ja|二階堂神社}} and carried through Sukagawa City to the event site at {{ill|Midori-gaoka Park|ja|翠ヶ丘公園}}. * In {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}}, the festival was held a month earlier on October 14th to coincide with the "Fukushima Kokutai" event. * In {{ill|2011|ja|2011th year }}, due to the effects of the {{ill|Fukushima nuclear accident|ja|福島第一原子力発電所事故}}, local {{ill|Torreya nucifera|lt=kaya grass|ja|カヤ}} used for the torches was found to contain trace amounts of radioactive material. Materials were sourced from across Japan to continue the festival.<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 'Fukushima's Sukagawa Hosts Taimatsu Akashi with Nationwide Material Support'] (Asahi.com, November 12, 2011)</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 'Flames of Recovery Brighten Fukushima - Taimatsu Akashi Held'] (Yomiuri Online, November 13, 2011)</ref> * The {{ill|2020|ja|2020年}} and {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year }} festivals were held without spectators to prevent the spread of COVID-19.
== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}} via the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}} (transfer at {{ill|Koriyama Station (Fukushima)|ja|郡山駅}}), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台}} or {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Sukagawa Station|ja|須賀川駅}} (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}}. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.

[[File:Kuriyasawa, Sukagawa, Fukushima Prefecture 962-0866, Japan - panoramio.jpg|thumb|280px|Taimatsu Akashi]] '''Taimatsu Akashi''' (松明あかし) is a {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, with a history spanning over 430 years. It is sometimes counted among {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り}}. The festival takes place annually on the second Saturday of {{ill|November|ja|11月}}.
== Overview == The festival is organized by the Sukagawa City Chamber of Commerce and features large {{ill|torches|ja|松明}} lit as a prayer for protection against disasters and a memorial for those who perished in historical battles.
== History == * In {{ill|1589|ja|1589年}} ({{ill|Tenshō 17|ja|天正17年}}), {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}} expanded his influence into the {{ill|Iwase District|ja|岩瀬郡}} region and demanded the surrender of the {{ill|Nikaidō clan|ja|二階堂氏}} at {{ill|Sukagawa Castle|ja|須賀川城}}. Enraged by this, the retainers and residents gathered on October 10th with torches in hand at {{ill|Tokayama|ja|十日山}}, east of the town, pledging to defend the castle at all costs. This event is considered the origin of the festival. * After the fall of Sukagawa Castle, the festival evolved into a ceremony to console the spirits of those who died in battle. Under new feudal rule, the festival was disguised as a "Mujina Hunt" to avoid interference from the rulers.<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm Sukagawa Tourism Association - Origin of Taimatsu Akashi]</ref> * The festival was historically held on October 10th of the lunar calendar and later shifted to November 10th of the solar calendar. Since {{ill|1986|ja|1986年}}, it has been held on the second Saturday of November. * The sacred fire is taken from {{ill|Nikaido Shrine|ja|二階堂神社}} and carried through Sukagawa City to the event site at {{ill|Midori-gaoka Park|ja|翠ヶ丘公園}}. * In {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year }}, the festival was held a month earlier on October 14th to coincide with the "Fukushima Kokutai" event. * In {{ill|2011|ja|2011th year }}, due to the effects of the {{ill|Fukushima nuclear accident|ja|福島第一原子力発電所事故}}, local {{ill|Torreya nucifera|lt=kaya grass|ja|カヤ}} used for the torches was found to contain trace amounts of radioactive material. Materials were sourced from across Japan to continue the festival.<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 'Fukushima's Sukagawa Hosts Taimatsu Akashi with Nationwide Material Support'] (Asahi.com, November 12, 2011)</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 'Flames of Recovery Brighten Fukushima - Taimatsu Akashi Held'] (Yomiuri Online, November 13, 2011)</ref> * The {{ill|2020|ja|2020年}} and {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year }} festivals were held without spectators to prevent the spread of COVID-19.
== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}} via the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}} (transfer at {{ill|Koriyama Station (Fukushima)|ja|郡山駅}}), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台}} or {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Sukagawa Station|ja|須賀川駅}} (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}}. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.

[[File:Kuriyasawa, Sukagawa, Fukushima Prefecture 962-0866, Japan - panoramio.jpg|thumb|280px|Taimatsu Akashi]] '''Taimatsu Akashi''' (松明あかし) is a {{ill|fire festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, with a history spanning over 430 years. It is sometimes counted among {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り}}. The festival takes place annually on the second Saturday of {{ill|November|ja|11月}}.
== Overview == The festival is organized by the Sukagawa City Chamber of Commerce and features large {{ill|torches|ja|松明}} lit as a prayer for protection against disasters and a memorial for those who perished in historical battles.
== History == * In {{ill|1589|ja|1589th year }} ({{ill|Tenshō 17|ja|天正17th year }}), {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}} expanded his influence into the {{ill|Iwase District|ja|岩瀬郡}} region and demanded the surrender of the {{ill|Nikaidō clan|ja|二階堂氏}} at {{ill|Sukagawa Castle|ja|須賀川城}}. Enraged by this, the retainers and residents gathered on October 10th with torches in hand at {{ill|Tokayama|ja|十日山}}, east of the town, pledging to defend the castle at all costs. This event is considered the origin of the festival. * After the fall of Sukagawa Castle, the festival evolved into a ceremony to console the spirits of those who died in battle. Under new feudal rule, the festival was disguised as a "Mujina Hunt" to avoid interference from the rulers.<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm Sukagawa Tourism Association - Origin of Taimatsu Akashi]</ref> * The festival was historically held on October 10th of the lunar calendar and later shifted to November 10th of the solar calendar. Since {{ill|1986|ja|1986年}}, it has been held on the second Saturday of November. * The sacred fire is taken from {{ill|Nikaido Shrine|ja|二階堂神社}} and carried through Sukagawa City to the event site at {{ill|Midori-gaoka Park|ja|翠ヶ丘公園}}. * In {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year }}, the festival was held a month earlier on October 14th to coincide with the "Fukushima Kokutai" event. * In {{ill|2011|ja|2011th year }}, due to the effects of the {{ill|Fukushima nuclear accident|ja|福島第一原子力発電所事故}}, local {{ill|Torreya nucifera|lt=kaya grass|ja|カヤ}} used for the torches was found to contain trace amounts of radioactive material. Materials were sourced from across Japan to continue the festival.<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 'Fukushima's Sukagawa Hosts Taimatsu Akashi with Nationwide Material Support'] (Asahi.com, November 12, 2011)</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 'Flames of Recovery Brighten Fukushima - Taimatsu Akashi Held'] (Yomiuri Online, November 13, 2011)</ref> * The {{ill|2020|ja|2020th year }} and {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year }} festivals were held without spectators to prevent the spread of COVID-19.
== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京}} via the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}} (transfer at {{ill|Koriyama Station (Fukushima)|ja|郡山駅}}), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台}} or {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}. * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Sukagawa Station|ja|須賀川駅}} (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}}. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Fire festival|ja|火祭り}} * {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|福島県須賀川市}} * {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/taimatsu/ Taimatsu Akashi (Sukagawa City)]
{{Event-stub}}

revision.text:
[[File:Kuriyasawa, Sukagawa, Fukushima Prefecture 962-0866, Japan - panoramio.jpg|thumb|280px|松明あかし]] '''松明あかし'''（たいまつあかし）とは、[[福島県]][[須賀川市]]で430年続く[[火祭り]]である。日本三大[[火祭り]]の一つに数えられることがある。毎年[[11月]]の第2土曜日に開催される。
== 概要 == [http://syeg.jp/taimatsu/ 須賀川市商工]
== 歴史 == * [[1589年]]（[[天正]]17年）[[伊達政宗]]が[[岩瀬郡]]周辺に勢力を伸ばして[[須賀川城]]の[[二階堂氏]]にも降伏を迫った。これに怒った家臣や領民が[[10月10日]]の夜、手に松明をともして町の東にある十日山に集まり、決死の覚悟で城を守ることを決議して城主の[[阿南姫|大乗院]]に進言した。この[[故事]]が祭りの元となる。 * 須賀川城の落城後は、この戦いで戦死した多くの人々の霊を弔うための行事として祭りが続けられた。須賀川周辺が新しい領主の支配下に入った後は領主の目をはばかり、ムジナ狩りと称して続けられ<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm 須賀川観光協会 - 松明あかしの由来]</ref>、現在に至る。 * かつては旧暦の10月10日、その後は[[1985年]]までは新暦の[[11月10日]]に開催されていたが、[[1986年]]以降は現在の11月第2土曜日に開催されている。 * 御神火は[[二階堂神社]]で採火され、須賀川市内を巡り、会場の翠ヶ丘公園へ運ばれる。 * [[1995年]]は「ふくしま国体」の開催に合わせて、通常より1か月早い[[10月14日]]に開催された。 * [[2011年]]の[[福島第一原子力発電所事故]]の影響により、祭に使用する地元産の[[カヤ]]から微量の[[放射性物質]]が検出された。須賀川市は[[福島第一原子力発電所|原発]]から約60kmの所に位置しており、全国に材料の提供を求めた<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 『福島・須賀川で「松明あかし」 全国から材料の支援』]（[[asahi.com]] 2011年11月12日）</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 『復興の炎、天まで届け…福島で「松明あかし」』]（[[YOMIURI ONLINE]] 2011年11月13日）</ref> 。 * [[2020年]]・[[2021年]]は新型コロナウイルス感染拡大防止のため無観客で実施している。
== 交通アクセス == * [[須賀川市]]までは[[東北新幹線]][[郡山駅 (福島県)|郡山]]経由で[[東京]]から約1時間、[[仙台]]・[[宇都宮市|宇都宮]]からは約1時間40分。 * [[JR]][[須賀川駅]]から徒歩15分 * [[東北自動車道]]の[[須賀川インターチェンジ]]から車で5分 * 会場周辺に駐車場が約2,000台分ある
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[火祭り]] * [[須賀川市|福島県須賀川市]] * [[日本三大一覧]] 「[[日本三大一覧#祭り|日本三大火祭り]]」
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/taimatsu/ 松明あかし（須賀川市）]
{{Event-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たいまつあかし}} [[Category:福島県の祭り]] [[Category:須賀川市]]

== 交通アクセス == * [[須賀川市]]までは[[東北新幹線]][[郡山駅 (福島県)|郡山]]経由で[[東京]]から約1時間、[[仙台]]・[[宇都宮市|宇都宮]]からは約1時間40分。 * [[JR]][[須賀川駅]]から徒歩15分 * [[東北自動車道]]の[[須賀川インターチェンジ]]から車で5分 * 会場周辺に駐車場が約2,000台分ある

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松明あかし]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11530743]] Wikidata name: Taimatsu Akashi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kuriyasawa, Sukagawa, Fukushima Prefecture 962-0866, Japan - panoramio.jpg|thumb|280px|Taimatsu Akashi]] '''Taimatsu Akashi''' (松明あかし) is a [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] held in [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], with a history spanning over 430 years. It is sometimes counted among {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The festival takes place annually on the second Saturday of [[:en:November|November]].
== Overview == The festival is organized by the Sukagawa City Chamber of Commerce and features large {{ill|torches|ja|松明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torches|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to たいまつ|1=torch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327954}} lit as a prayer for protection against disasters and a memorial for those who perished in historical battles.
== History == * In [[:en:1589|1589]] ({{ill|Tenshō 17|ja|天正17年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 17|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Date Masamune|Date Masamune]] expanded his influence into the [[:en:Iwase District, Fukushima|Iwase District]] region and demanded the surrender of the [[:en:Nikaidō clan|Nikaidō clan]] at {{ill|Sukagawa Castle|ja|須賀川城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Castle|qq=|1=Sukagawa Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664794}}. Enraged by this, the retainers and residents gathered on October 10th with torches in hand at {{ill|Tokayama|ja|十日山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokayama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, east of the town, pledging to defend the castle at all costs. This event is considered the origin of the festival. * After the fall of Sukagawa Castle, the festival evolved into a ceremony to console the spirits of those who died in battle. Under new feudal rule, the festival was disguised as a "Mujina Hunt" to avoid interference from the rulers.<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm Sukagawa Tourism Association - Origin of Taimatsu Akashi]</ref> * The festival was historically held on October 10th of the lunar calendar and later shifted to November 10th of the solar calendar. Since [[:en:1986|1986]], it has been held on the second Saturday of November. * The sacred fire is taken from {{ill|Nikaido Shrine|ja|二階堂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikaido Shrine|qq=}} and carried through Sukagawa City to the event site at {{ill|Midori-gaoka Park|ja|翠ヶ丘公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori-gaoka Park|qq=|1=Midori-gaoka Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60988274}}. * In [[:en:1995|1995]], the festival was held a month earlier on October 14th to coincide with the "Fukushima Kokutai" event. * In [[:en:2011|2011]], due to the effects of the [[:en:Fukushima nuclear accident|Fukushima nuclear accident]], local [[:en:Torreya nucifera|kaya grass]] used for the torches was found to contain trace amounts of radioactive material. Materials were sourced from across Japan to continue the festival.<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 'Fukushima's Sukagawa Hosts Taimatsu Akashi with Nationwide Material Support'] (Asahi.com, November 12, 2011)</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 'Flames of Recovery Brighten Fukushima - Taimatsu Akashi Held'] (Yomiuri Online, November 13, 2011)</ref> * The [[:en:2020|2020]] and [[:en:2021|2021]] festivals were held without spectators to prevent the spread of COVID-19.
== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}} via the [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]] (transfer at [[:en:Kōriyama Station|Koriyama Station (Fukushima)]]), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|qq=|1=Sendai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377702}} or [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]]. * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sukagawa Station|Sukagawa Station]] (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tōhoku Expressway]]. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] * {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|福島県須賀川市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa, Fukushima|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/taimatsu/ Taimatsu Akashi (Sukagawa City)]

Since [[:en:1986|1986]], it has been held on the second Saturday of November. * The sacred fire is taken from {{ill|Nikaido Shrine|ja|二階堂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikaido Shrine|qq=}} and carried through Sukagawa City to the event site at {{ill|Midori-gaoka Park|ja|翠ヶ丘公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori-gaoka Park|qq=|1=Midori-gaoka Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60988274}}. * In [[:en:1995|1995]], the festival was held a month earlier on October 14th to coincide with the "Fukushima Kokutai" event. * In [[:en:2011|2011]], due to the effects of the [[:en:Fukushima nuclear accident|Fukushima nuclear accident]], local [[:en:Torreya nucifera|kaya grass]] used for the torches was found to contain trace amounts of radioactive material. Materials were sourced from across Japan to continue the festival.<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 'Fukushima's Sukagawa Hosts Taimatsu Akashi with Nationwide Material Support'] (Asahi.com, November 12, 2011)</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 'Flames of Recovery Brighten Fukushima - Taimatsu Akashi Held'] (Yomiuri Online, November 13, 2011)</ref> * The [[:en:2020|2020]] and [[:en:2021|2021]] festivals were held without spectators to prevent the spread of COVID-19.

Materials were sourced from across Japan to continue the festival.<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 'Fukushima's Sukagawa Hosts Taimatsu Akashi with Nationwide Material Support'] (Asahi.com, November 12, 2011)</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 'Flames of Recovery Brighten Fukushima - Taimatsu Akashi Held'] (Yomiuri Online, November 13, 2011)</ref> * The [[:en:2020|2020]] and [[:en:2021|2021]] festivals were held without spectators to prevent the spread of COVID-19.
== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}} via the [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]] (transfer at [[:en:Kōriyama Station|Koriyama Station (Fukushima)]]), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|1=Sendai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377702}} or [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]]. * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sukagawa Station|Sukagawa Station]] (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tōhoku Expressway]]. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松明あかし]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11530743]] Wikidata name: Taimatsu Akashi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kuriyasawa, Sukagawa, Fukushima Prefecture 962-0866, Japan - panoramio.jpg|thumb|280px|Taimatsu Akashi]] '''Taimatsu Akashi''' (松明あかし) is a [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] held in [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], with a history spanning over 430 years. It is sometimes counted among {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The festival takes place annually on the second Saturday of [[:en:November|November]].
== Overview == The festival is organized by the Sukagawa City Chamber of Commerce and features large {{ill|torches|ja|松明|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torches|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to たいまつ|1=torch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327954}} lit as a prayer for protection against disasters and a memorial for those who perished in historical battles.
== History == * In [[:en:1589|1589]] ({{ill|Tenshō 17|ja|天正17年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 17|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Date Masamune|Date Masamune]] expanded his influence into the [[:en:Iwase District, Fukushima|Iwase District]] region and demanded the surrender of the [[:en:Nikaidō clan|Nikaidō clan]] at {{ill|Sukagawa Castle|ja|須賀川城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Castle|1=Sukagawa Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664794}}. Enraged by this, the retainers and residents gathered on October 10th with torches in hand at {{ill|Tokayama|ja|十日山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, east of the town, pledging to defend the castle at all costs. This event is considered the origin of the festival. * After the fall of Sukagawa Castle, the festival evolved into a ceremony to console the spirits of those who died in battle. Under new feudal rule, the festival was disguised as a "Mujina Hunt" to avoid interference from the rulers.<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm Sukagawa Tourism Association - Origin of Taimatsu Akashi]</ref> * The festival was historically held on October 10th of the lunar calendar and later shifted to November 10th of the solar calendar. Since [[:en:1986|1986]], it has been held on the second Saturday of November. * The sacred fire is taken from {{ill|Nikaido Shrine|ja|二階堂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikaido Shrine|qq=}} and carried through Sukagawa City to the event site at {{ill|Midori-gaoka Park|ja|翠ヶ丘公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori-gaoka Park|1=Midori-gaoka Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60988274}}. * In [[:en:1995|1995]], the festival was held a month earlier on October 14th to coincide with the "Fukushima Kokutai" event. * In [[:en:2011|2011]], due to the effects of the [[:en:Fukushima nuclear accident|Fukushima nuclear accident]], local [[:en:Torreya nucifera|kaya grass]] used for the torches was found to contain trace amounts of radioactive material. Materials were sourced from across Japan to continue the festival.<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 'Fukushima's Sukagawa Hosts Taimatsu Akashi with Nationwide Material Support'] (Asahi.com, November 12, 2011)</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 'Flames of Recovery Brighten Fukushima - Taimatsu Akashi Held'] (Yomiuri Online, November 13, 2011)</ref> * The [[:en:2020|2020]] and [[:en:2021|2021]] festivals were held without spectators to prevent the spread of COVID-19.
== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}} via the [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]] (transfer at [[:en:Kōriyama Station|Koriyama Station (Fukushima)]]), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|1=Sendai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377702}} or [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]]. * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sukagawa Station|Sukagawa Station]] (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tōhoku Expressway]]. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.

== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}} via the [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]] (transfer at [[:en:Kōriyama Station|Koriyama Station (Fukushima)]]), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|1=Sendai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377702}} or [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]]. * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sukagawa Station|Sukagawa Station]] (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tōhoku Expressway]]. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松明あかし]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11530743]] Wikidata name: Taimatsu Akashi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kuriyasawa, Sukagawa, Fukushima Prefecture 962-0866, Japan - panoramio.jpg|thumb|280px|Taimatsu Akashi]] '''Taimatsu Akashi''' (松明あかし) is a [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] held in [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], with a history spanning over 430 years. It is sometimes counted among {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The festival takes place annually on the second Saturday of [[:en:November|November]].
== Overview == The festival is organized by the Sukagawa City Chamber of Commerce and features large {{ill|torches|ja|松明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torches|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to たいまつ|1=torch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327954}} lit as a prayer for protection against disasters and a memorial for those who perished in historical battles.
== History == * In [[:en:1589|1589]] ({{ill|Tenshō 17|ja|天正17年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 17|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Date Masamune|Date Masamune]] expanded his influence into the [[:en:Iwase District, Fukushima|Iwase District]] region and demanded the surrender of the [[:en:Nikaidō clan|Nikaidō clan]] at {{ill|Sukagawa Castle|ja|須賀川城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Castle|1=Sukagawa Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664794}}. Enraged by this, the retainers and residents gathered on October 10th with torches in hand at {{ill|Tokayama|ja|十日山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, east of the town, pledging to defend the castle at all costs. This event is considered the origin of the festival. * After the fall of Sukagawa Castle, the festival evolved into a ceremony to console the spirits of those who died in battle. Under new feudal rule, the festival was disguised as a "Mujina Hunt" to avoid interference from the rulers.<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm Sukagawa Tourism Association - Origin of Taimatsu Akashi]</ref> * The festival was historically held on October 10th of the lunar calendar and later shifted to November 10th of the solar calendar. Since [[:en:1986|1986]], it has been held on the second Saturday of November. * The sacred fire is taken from {{ill|Nikaido Shrine|ja|二階堂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikaido Shrine|qq=}} and carried through Sukagawa City to the event site at {{ill|Midori-gaoka Park|ja|翠ヶ丘公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori-gaoka Park|1=Midori-gaoka Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60988274}}. * In [[:en:1995|1995]], the festival was held a month earlier on October 14th to coincide with the "Fukushima Kokutai" event. * In [[:en:2011|2011]], due to the effects of the [[:en:Fukushima nuclear accident|Fukushima nuclear accident]], local [[:en:Torreya nucifera|kaya grass]] used for the torches was found to contain trace amounts of radioactive material. Materials were sourced from across Japan to continue the festival.<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 'Fukushima's Sukagawa Hosts Taimatsu Akashi with Nationwide Material Support'] (Asahi.com, November 12, 2011)</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 'Flames of Recovery Brighten Fukushima - Taimatsu Akashi Held'] (Yomiuri Online, November 13, 2011)</ref> * The [[:en:2020|2020]] and [[:en:2021|2021]] festivals were held without spectators to prevent the spread of COVID-19.
== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}} via the [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]] (transfer at [[:en:Kōriyama Station|Koriyama Station (Fukushima)]]), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|1=Sendai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377702}} or [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]]. * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sukagawa Station|Sukagawa Station]] (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tōhoku Expressway]]. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.

== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}} via the [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]] (transfer at [[:en:Kōriyama Station|Koriyama Station (Fukushima)]]), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|1=Sendai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377702}} or [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]]. * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sukagawa Station|Sukagawa Station]] (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tōhoku Expressway]]. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.
== References ==

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松明あかし]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11530743]] Wikidata name: Taimatsu Akashi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kuriyasawa, Sukagawa, Fukushima Prefecture 962-0866, Japan - panoramio.jpg|thumb|280px|Taimatsu Akashi]] '''Taimatsu Akashi''' (松明あかし) is a [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] held in [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], with a history spanning over 430 years. It is sometimes counted among {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The festival takes place annually on the second Saturday of [[:en:November|November]].
== Overview == The festival is organized by the Sukagawa City Chamber of Commerce and features large {{ill|torches|ja|松明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torches|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to たいまつ|1=torch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327954}} lit as a prayer for protection against disasters and a memorial for those who perished in historical battles.
== History == * In [[:en:1589|1589]] ({{ill|Tenshō 17|ja|天正17年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 17|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Date Masamune|Date Masamune]] expanded his influence into the [[:en:Iwase District, Fukushima|Iwase District]] region and demanded the surrender of the [[:en:Nikaidō clan|Nikaidō clan]] at {{ill|Sukagawa Castle|ja|須賀川城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Castle|1=Sukagawa Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664794}}. Enraged by this, the retainers and residents gathered on October 10th with torches in hand at {{ill|Tokayama|ja|十日山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, east of the town, pledging to defend the castle at all costs. This event is considered the origin of the festival. * After the fall of Sukagawa Castle, the festival evolved into a ceremony to console the spirits of those who died in battle. Under new feudal rule, the festival was disguised as a "Mujina Hunt" to avoid interference from the rulers.<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm Sukagawa Tourism Association - Origin of Taimatsu Akashi]</ref> * The festival was historically held on October 10th of the lunar calendar and later shifted to November 10th of the solar calendar. Since [[:en:1986|1986]], it has been held on the second Saturday of November. * The sacred fire is taken from {{ill|Nikaido Shrine|ja|二階堂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikaido Shrine|qq=}} and carried through Sukagawa City to the event site at {{ill|Midori-gaoka Park|ja|翠ヶ丘公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori-gaoka Park|1=Midori-gaoka Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60988274}}. * In [[:en:1995|1995]], the festival was held a month earlier on October 14th to coincide with the "Fukushima Kokutai" event. * In [[:en:2011|2011]], due to the effects of the [[:en:Fukushima nuclear accident|Fukushima nuclear accident]], local [[:en:Torreya nucifera|kaya grass]] used for the torches was found to contain trace amounts of radioactive material. Materials were sourced from across Japan to continue the festival.<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 'Fukushima's Sukagawa Hosts Taimatsu Akashi with Nationwide Material Support'] (Asahi.com, November 12, 2011)</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 'Flames of Recovery Brighten Fukushima - Taimatsu Akashi Held'] (Yomiuri Online, November 13, 2011)</ref> * The [[:en:2020|2020]] and [[:en:2021|2021]] festivals were held without spectators to prevent the spread of COVID-19.
== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}} via the [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]] (transfer at [[:en:Kōriyama Station|Koriyama Station (Fukushima)]]), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|1=Sendai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377702}} or [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]]. * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sukagawa Station|Sukagawa Station]] (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tōhoku Expressway]]. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] * {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|福島県須賀川市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/taimatsu/ Taimatsu Akashi (Sukagawa City)]

Materials were sourced from across Japan to continue the festival.<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 'Fukushima's Sukagawa Hosts Taimatsu Akashi with Nationwide Material Support'] (Asahi.com, November 12, 2011)</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 'Flames of Recovery Brighten Fukushima - Taimatsu Akashi Held'] (Yomiuri Online, November 13, 2011)</ref> * The [[:en:2020|2020]] and [[:en:2021|2021]] festivals were held without spectators to prevent the spread of COVID-19.
== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}} via the [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]] (transfer at [[:en:Kōriyama Station|Koriyama Station (Fukushima)]]), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|1=Sendai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377702}} or [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]]. * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sukagawa Station|Sukagawa Station]] (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tōhoku Expressway]]. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松明あかし]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松明あかし]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11530743]] Wikidata name: Taimatsu Akashi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kuriyasawa, Sukagawa, Fukushima Prefecture 962-0866, Japan - panoramio.jpg|thumb|280px|Taimatsu Akashi]] '''Taimatsu Akashi''' (松明あかし) is a [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] held in [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], with a history spanning over 430 years. It is sometimes counted among {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The festival takes place annually on the second Saturday of [[:en:November|November]].
== Overview == The festival is organized by the Sukagawa City Chamber of Commerce and features large {{ill|torches|ja|松明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torches|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to たいまつ|1=torch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327954}} lit as a prayer for protection against disasters and a memorial for those who perished in historical battles.
== History == * In [[:en:1589|1589]] ({{ill|Tenshō 17|ja|天正17年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 17|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Date Masamune|Date Masamune]] expanded his influence into the [[:en:Iwase District, Fukushima|Iwase District]] region and demanded the surrender of the [[:en:Nikaidō clan|Nikaidō clan]] at {{ill|Sukagawa Castle|ja|須賀川城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Castle|1=Sukagawa Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664794}}. Enraged by this, the retainers and residents gathered on October 10th with torches in hand at {{ill|Tokayama|ja|十日山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, east of the town, pledging to defend the castle at all costs. This event is considered the origin of the festival. * After the fall of Sukagawa Castle, the festival evolved into a ceremony to console the spirits of those who died in battle. Under new feudal rule, the festival was disguised as a "Mujina Hunt" to avoid interference from the rulers.<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm Sukagawa Tourism Association - Origin of Taimatsu Akashi]</ref> * The festival was historically held on October 10th of the lunar calendar and later shifted to November 10th of the solar calendar. Since [[:en:1986|1986]], it has been held on the second Saturday of November. * The sacred fire is taken from {{ill|Nikaido Shrine|ja|二階堂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikaido Shrine|qq=}} and carried through Sukagawa City to the event site at {{ill|Midori-gaoka Park|ja|翠ヶ丘公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori-gaoka Park|1=Midori-gaoka Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60988274}}. * In [[:en:1995|1995]], the festival was held a month earlier on October 14th to coincide with the "Fukushima Kokutai" event. * In [[:en:2011|2011]], due to the effects of the [[:en:Fukushima nuclear accident|Fukushima nuclear accident]], local [[:en:Torreya nucifera|kaya grass]] used for the torches was found to contain trace amounts of radioactive material. Materials were sourced from across Japan to continue the festival.<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 'Fukushima's Sukagawa Hosts Taimatsu Akashi with Nationwide Material Support'] (Asahi.com, November 12, 2011)</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 'Flames of Recovery Brighten Fukushima - Taimatsu Akashi Held'] (Yomiuri Online, November 13, 2011)</ref> * The [[:en:2020|2020]] and [[:en:2021|2021]] festivals were held without spectators to prevent the spread of COVID-19.
== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}} via the [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]] (transfer at [[:en:Kōriyama Station|Koriyama Station (Fukushima)]]), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|1=Sendai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377702}} or [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]]. * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sukagawa Station|Sukagawa Station]] (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tōhoku Expressway]]. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.

== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}} via the [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]] (transfer at [[:en:Kōriyama Station|Koriyama Station (Fukushima)]]), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|1=Sendai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377702}} or [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]]. * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sukagawa Station|Sukagawa Station]] (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tōhoku Expressway]]. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] * {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|福島県須賀川市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11530743}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Festivals in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Sukagawa, Fukushima]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松明あかし]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松明あかし]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松明あかし]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11530743]] Wikidata name: Taimatsu Akashi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kuriyasawa, Sukagawa, Fukushima Prefecture 962-0866, Japan - panoramio.jpg|thumb|280px|Taimatsu Akashi]] '''Taimatsu Akashi''' (松明あかし) is a [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] held in [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], with a history spanning over 430 years. It is sometimes counted among {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The festival takes place annually on the second Saturday of [[:en:November|November]].
== Overview == The festival is organized by the Sukagawa City Chamber of Commerce and features large {{ill|torches|ja|松明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torches|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to たいまつ|1=torch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327954}} lit as a prayer for protection against disasters and a memorial for those who perished in historical battles.
== History == * In [[:en:1589|1589]] ({{ill|Tenshō 17|ja|天正17年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 17|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Date Masamune|Date Masamune]] expanded his influence into the [[:en:Iwase District, Fukushima|Iwase District]] region and demanded the surrender of the [[:en:Nikaidō clan|Nikaidō clan]] at {{ill|Sukagawa Castle|ja|須賀川城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Castle|1=Sukagawa Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664794}}. Enraged by this, the retainers and residents gathered on October 10th with torches in hand at {{ill|Tokayama|ja|十日山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, east of the town, pledging to defend the castle at all costs. This event is considered the origin of the festival. * After the fall of Sukagawa Castle, the festival evolved into a ceremony to console the spirits of those who died in battle. Under new feudal rule, the festival was disguised as a "Mujina Hunt" to avoid interference from the rulers.<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm Sukagawa Tourism Association - Origin of Taimatsu Akashi]</ref> * The festival was historically held on October 10th of the lunar calendar and later shifted to November 10th of the solar calendar. Since [[:en:1986|1986]], it has been held on the second Saturday of November. * The sacred fire is taken from {{ill|Nikaido Shrine|ja|二階堂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikaido Shrine|qq=}} and carried through Sukagawa City to the event site at {{ill|Midori-gaoka Park|ja|翠ヶ丘公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori-gaoka Park|1=Midori-gaoka Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60988274}}. * In [[:en:1995|1995]], the festival was held a month earlier on October 14th to coincide with the "Fukushima Kokutai" event. * In [[:en:2011|2011]], due to the effects of the [[:en:Fukushima nuclear accident|Fukushima nuclear accident]], local [[:en:Torreya nucifera|kaya grass]] used for the torches was found to contain trace amounts of radioactive material. Materials were sourced from across Japan to continue the festival.<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 'Fukushima's Sukagawa Hosts Taimatsu Akashi with Nationwide Material Support'] (Asahi.com, November 12, 2011)</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 'Flames of Recovery Brighten Fukushima - Taimatsu Akashi Held'] (Yomiuri Online, November 13, 2011)</ref> * The [[:en:2020|2020]] and [[:en:2021|2021]] festivals were held without spectators to prevent the spread of COVID-19.
== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}} via the [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]] (transfer at [[:en:Kōriyama Station|Koriyama Station (Fukushima)]]), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|1=Sendai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377702}} or [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]]. * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Sukagawa Station|Sukagawa Station]] (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} on the [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tōhoku Expressway]]. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] * {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|福島県須賀川市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

Since {{ill|1986|en|1986|WD=Q2430|lt=1986}}, it has been held on the second Saturday of November. * The sacred fire is taken from {{ill|Nikaido Shrine|ja|二階堂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikaido Shrine|qq=}} and carried through Sukagawa City to the event site at {{ill|Midori-gaoka Park|ja|翠ヶ丘公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori-gaoka Park|1=Midori-gaoka Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60988274}}. * In {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}}, the festival was held a month earlier on October 14th to coincide with the "Fukushima Kokutai" event. * In {{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011}}, due to the effects of the {{ill|Fukushima nuclear accident|en|Fukushima nuclear accident|WD=Q171178|lt=Fukushima nuclear accident}}, local {{ill|Torreya nucifera|en|Torreya nucifera|WD=Q1373999|lt=kaya grass}} used for the torches was found to contain trace amounts of radioactive material. Materials were sourced from across Japan to continue the festival.<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 'Fukushima's Sukagawa Hosts Taimatsu Akashi with Nationwide Material Support'] (Asahi.com, November 12, 2011)</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 'Flames of Recovery Brighten Fukushima - Taimatsu Akashi Held'] (Yomiuri Online, November 13, 2011)</ref> * The {{ill|2020|en|2020|WD=Q25337|lt=2020}} and {{ill|2021|en|2021|WD=Q49628|lt=2021}} festivals were held without spectators to prevent the spread of COVID-19.
== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}} via the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|en|Tōhoku Shinkansen|WD=Q900665|lt=Tōhoku Shinkansen}} (transfer at {{ill|Kōriyama Station|en|Kōriyama Station|WD=Q1208435|lt=Koriyama Station (Fukushima)}}), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|1=Sendai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377702}} or {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya}}. * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Sukagawa Station|en|Sukagawa Station|WD=Q876991|lt=Sukagawa Station}} (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tōhoku Expressway}}. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|WD=Q3135724|lt=Fire festival}} * {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|福島県須賀川市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/taimatsu/ Taimatsu Akashi (Sukagawa City)]
