<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松明あかし]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松明あかし]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松明あかし]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11530743]] Wikidata name: Taimatsu Akashi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kuriyasawa, Sukagawa, Fukushima Prefecture 962-0866, Japan - panoramio.jpg|thumb|280px|Taimatsu Akashi]] '''Taimatsu Akashi''' (松明あかし) is a {{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|WD=Q3135724|lt=Fire festival}} held in {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|en|Sukagawa, Fukushima|WD=Q819664|lt=Sukagawa, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}, with a history spanning over 430 years. It is sometimes counted among {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The festival takes place annually on the second Saturday of {{ill|November|en|November|WD=Q125|lt=November}}.
== Overview == The festival is organized by the Sukagawa City Chamber of Commerce and features large {{ill|torches|ja|松明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torches|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to たいまつ|1=torch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327954}} lit as a prayer for protection against disasters and a memorial for those who perished in historical battles.
== History == * In {{ill|1589|en|1589|WD=Q6759|lt=1589}} ({{ill|Tenshō 17|ja|天正17年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 17|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), {{ill|Date Masamune|en|Date Masamune|WD=Q311183|lt=Date Masamune}} expanded his influence into the {{ill|Iwase District, Fukushima|en|Iwase District, Fukushima|WD=Q1155686|lt=Iwase District}} region and demanded the surrender of the {{ill|Nikaidō clan|en|Nikaidō clan|WD=Q2000952|lt=Nikaidō clan}} at {{ill|Sukagawa Castle|ja|須賀川城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Castle|1=Sukagawa Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664794}}. Enraged by this, the retainers and residents gathered on October 10th with torches in hand at {{ill|Tokayama|ja|十日山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, east of the town, pledging to defend the castle at all costs. This event is considered the origin of the festival. * After the fall of Sukagawa Castle, the festival evolved into a ceremony to console the spirits of those who died in battle. Under new feudal rule, the festival was disguised as a "Mujina Hunt" to avoid interference from the rulers.<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm Sukagawa Tourism Association - Origin of Taimatsu Akashi]</ref> * The festival was historically held on October 10th of the lunar calendar and later shifted to November 10th of the solar calendar. Since {{ill|1986|en|1986|WD=Q2430|lt=1986}}, it has been held on the second Saturday of November. * The sacred fire is taken from {{ill|Nikaido Shrine|ja|二階堂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikaido Shrine|qq=}} and carried through Sukagawa City to the event site at {{ill|Midori-gaoka Park|ja|翠ヶ丘公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori-gaoka Park|1=Midori-gaoka Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60988274}}. * In {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}}, the festival was held a month earlier on October 14th to coincide with the "Fukushima Kokutai" event. * In {{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011}}, due to the effects of the {{ill|Fukushima nuclear accident|en|Fukushima nuclear accident|WD=Q171178|lt=Fukushima nuclear accident}}, local {{ill|Torreya nucifera|en|Torreya nucifera|WD=Q1373999|lt=kaya grass}} used for the torches was found to contain trace amounts of radioactive material. Materials were sourced from across Japan to continue the festival.<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 'Fukushima's Sukagawa Hosts Taimatsu Akashi with Nationwide Material Support'] (Asahi.com, November 12, 2011)</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 'Flames of Recovery Brighten Fukushima - Taimatsu Akashi Held'] (Yomiuri Online, November 13, 2011)</ref> * The {{ill|2020|en|2020|WD=Q25337|lt=2020}} and {{ill|2021|en|2021|WD=Q49628|lt=2021}} festivals were held without spectators to prevent the spread of COVID-19.
== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}} via the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|en|Tōhoku Shinkansen|WD=Q900665|lt=Tōhoku Shinkansen}} (transfer at {{ill|Kōriyama Station|en|Kōriyama Station|WD=Q1208435|lt=Koriyama Station (Fukushima)}}), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|1=Sendai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377702}} or {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya}}. * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Sukagawa Station|en|Sukagawa Station|WD=Q876991|lt=Sukagawa Station}} (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tōhoku Expressway}}. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.

[[File:Kuriyasawa, Sukagawa, Fukushima Prefecture 962-0866, Japan - panoramio.jpg|thumb|280px|Taimatsu Akashi]] '''Taimatsu Akashi''' (松明あかし) is a {{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|WD=Q3135724|lt=Fire festival}} held in {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|en|Sukagawa, Fukushima|WD=Q819664|lt=Sukagawa, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}, with a history spanning over 430 years. It is sometimes counted among {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The festival takes place annually on the second Saturday of {{ill|November|en|November|WD=Q125|lt=November}}.
== Overview == The festival is organized by the Sukagawa City Chamber of Commerce and features large {{ill|torches|ja|松明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torches|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to たいまつ|1=torch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327954}} lit as a prayer for protection against disasters and a memorial for those who perished in historical battles.
== History == * In {{ill|1589|en|1589|WD=Q6759|lt=1589}} ({{ill|Tenshō 17|ja|天正17年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 17|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), {{ill|Date Masamune|en|Date Masamune|WD=Q311183|lt=Date Masamune}} expanded his influence into the {{ill|Iwase District, Fukushima|en|Iwase District, Fukushima|WD=Q1155686|lt=Iwase District}} region and demanded the surrender of the {{ill|Nikaidō clan|en|Nikaidō clan|WD=Q2000952|lt=Nikaidō clan}} at {{ill|Sukagawa Castle|ja|須賀川城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Castle|1=Sukagawa Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664794}}. Enraged by this, the retainers and residents gathered on October 10th with torches in hand at {{ill|Tokayama|ja|十日山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, east of the town, pledging to defend the castle at all costs. This event is considered the origin of the festival. * After the fall of Sukagawa Castle, the festival evolved into a ceremony to console the spirits of those who died in battle. Under new feudal rule, the festival was disguised as a "Mujina Hunt" to avoid interference from the rulers.<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm Sukagawa Tourism Association - Origin of Taimatsu Akashi]</ref> * The festival was historically held on October 10th of the lunar calendar and later shifted to November 10th of the solar calendar. Since {{ill|1986|en|1986|WD=Q2430|lt=1986}}, it has been held on the second Saturday of November. * The sacred fire is taken from {{ill|Nikaido Shrine|ja|二階堂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikaido Shrine|qq=}} and carried through Sukagawa City to the event site at {{ill|Midori-gaoka Park|ja|翠ヶ丘公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori-gaoka Park|1=Midori-gaoka Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60988274}}. * In {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}}, the festival was held a month earlier on October 14th to coincide with the "Fukushima Kokutai" event. * In {{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011}}, due to the effects of the {{ill|Fukushima nuclear accident|en|Fukushima nuclear accident|WD=Q171178|lt=Fukushima nuclear accident}}, local {{ill|Torreya nucifera|en|Torreya nucifera|WD=Q1373999|lt=kaya grass}} used for the torches was found to contain trace amounts of radioactive material. Materials were sourced from across Japan to continue the festival.<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 'Fukushima's Sukagawa Hosts Taimatsu Akashi with Nationwide Material Support'] (Asahi.com, November 12, 2011)</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 'Flames of Recovery Brighten Fukushima - Taimatsu Akashi Held'] (Yomiuri Online, November 13, 2011)</ref> * The {{ill|2020|en|2020|WD=Q25337|lt=2020}} and {{ill|2021|en|2021|WD=Q49628|lt=2021}} festivals were held without spectators to prevent the spread of COVID-19.
== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}} via the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|en|Tōhoku Shinkansen|WD=Q900665|lt=Tōhoku Shinkansen}} (transfer at {{ill|Kōriyama Station|en|Kōriyama Station|WD=Q1208435|lt=Koriyama Station (Fukushima)}}), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|1=Sendai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377702}} or {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya}}. * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Sukagawa Station|en|Sukagawa Station|WD=Q876991|lt=Sukagawa Station}} (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tōhoku Expressway}}. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|WD=Q3135724|lt=Fire festival}} * {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|福島県須賀川市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/taimatsu/ Taimatsu Akashi (Sukagawa City)]
{{Event-stub}}

== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo Proper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}} via the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|en|Tōhoku Shinkansen|WD=Q900665|lt=Tōhoku Shinkansen|1=Tōhoku Shinkansen}} (transfer at {{ill|Kōriyama Station|en|Kōriyama Station|WD=Q1208435|lt=Koriyama Station (Fukushima)|1=Kōriyama Station}}), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|1=Q11377702|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377702}} or {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}. * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Sukagawa Station|en|Sukagawa Station|WD=Q876991|lt=Sukagawa Station|1=Sukagawa Station}} (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tōhoku Expressway|1=Tōhoku Expressway}}. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.

[[File:Kuriyasawa, Sukagawa, Fukushima Prefecture 962-0866, Japan - panoramio.jpg|thumb|280px|Taimatsu Akashi]] '''Taimatsu Akashi''' (松明あかし) is a {{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|WD=Q3135724|lt=Fire festival|1=Fire Festival}} held in {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|en|Sukagawa, Fukushima|WD=Q819664|lt=Sukagawa, Fukushima|1=Sukagawa, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, with a history spanning over 430 years. It is sometimes counted among {{ill|Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja|日本三大火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Fire Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The festival takes place annually on the second Saturday of {{ill|November|en|November|WD=Q125|lt=November|1=November}}.
== Overview == The festival is organized by the Sukagawa City Chamber of Commerce and features large {{ill|torches|ja|松明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torches|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to たいまつ|1=Torch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327954}} lit as a prayer for protection against disasters and a memorial for those who perished in historical battles.
== History == * In {{ill|1589|en|1589|WD=Q6759|lt=1589|1=1589}} ({{ill|Tenshō 17|ja|天正17年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 17|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), {{ill|Date Masamune|en|Date Masamune|WD=Q311183|lt=Date Masamune|1=Date Masamune}} expanded his influence into the {{ill|Iwase District, Fukushima|en|Iwase District, Fukushima|WD=Q1155686|lt=Iwase District|1=Iwase District, Fukushima}} region and demanded the surrender of the {{ill|Nikaidō clan|en|Nikaidō clan|WD=Q2000952|lt=Nikaidō clan|1=Nikaidō clan}} at {{ill|Sukagawa Castle|ja|須賀川城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Castle|1=Q11664794|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664794}}. Enraged by this, the retainers and residents gathered on October 10th with torches in hand at {{ill|Tokayama|ja|十日山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, east of the town, pledging to defend the castle at all costs. This event is considered the origin of the festival. * After the fall of Sukagawa Castle, the festival evolved into a ceremony to console the spirits of those who died in battle. Under new feudal rule, the festival was disguised as a "Mujina Hunt" to avoid interference from the rulers.<ref>[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2727.htm Sukagawa Tourism Association - Origin of Taimatsu Akashi]</ref> * The festival was historically held on October 10th of the lunar calendar and later shifted to November 10th of the solar calendar. Since {{ill|1986|en|1986|WD=Q2430|lt=1986|1=1986}}, it has been held on the second Saturday of November. * The sacred fire is taken from {{ill|Nikaido Shrine|ja|二階堂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikaido Shrine|qq=}} and carried through Sukagawa City to the event site at {{ill|Midori-gaoka Park|ja|翠ヶ丘公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midori-gaoka Park|1=Q60988274|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60988274}}. * In {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}, the festival was held a month earlier on October 14th to coincide with the "Fukushima Kokutai" event. * In {{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011|1=2011}}, due to the effects of the {{ill|Fukushima nuclear accident|en|Fukushima nuclear accident|WD=Q171178|lt=Fukushima nuclear accident|1=Fukushima nuclear accident}}, local {{ill|Torreya nucifera|en|Torreya nucifera|WD=Q1373999|lt=kaya grass|1=Torreya nucifera}} used for the torches was found to contain trace amounts of radioactive material. Materials were sourced from across Japan to continue the festival.<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/national/update/1112/TKY201111120409.html 'Fukushima's Sukagawa Hosts Taimatsu Akashi with Nationwide Material Support'] (Asahi.com, November 12, 2011)</ref><ref>[http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/news/20111113-OYT1T00264.htm 'Flames of Recovery Brighten Fukushima - Taimatsu Akashi Held'] (Yomiuri Online, November 13, 2011)</ref> * The {{ill|2020|en|2020|WD=Q25337|lt=2020|1=2020}} and {{ill|2021|en|2021|WD=Q49628|lt=2021|1=2021}} festivals were held without spectators to prevent the spread of COVID-19.
== Access == * Sukagawa City is about 1 hour from {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|1=Tokyo Proper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7473516}} via the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|en|Tōhoku Shinkansen|WD=Q900665|lt=Tōhoku Shinkansen|1=Tōhoku Shinkansen}} (transfer at {{ill|Kōriyama Station|en|Kōriyama Station|WD=Q1208435|lt=Koriyama Station (Fukushima)|1=Kōriyama Station}}), and approximately 1 hour 40 minutes from {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|1=Q11377702|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377702}} or {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}. * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Sukagawa Station|en|Sukagawa Station|WD=Q876991|lt=Sukagawa Station|1=Sukagawa Station}} (JR Line). * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|WD=Q428428|lt=Tōhoku Expressway|1=Tōhoku Expressway}}. * Approximately 2,000 parking spaces are available near the venue.
